# DIY Manual

Practical command cookbook for running X2Chess end-to-end from terminal cells (including Quarto).

Use this from repository root unless a section says otherwise.

## Quick Start (Most Used)

> Note: Browser mode is not used in this project. X2Chess runs as a Tauri desktop client.
> Note: X2Chess frontend uses port `5287`.
> Note: Developer-Tools defaults ON in `desktop:dev` and OFF in packaged PROD builds.
> Note: The Developer Tools panel (AST view) starts closed in all modes and must be opened via the app sidebar.

### 1) Check and remove stale dev processes (recommended before start)

```{bash}
# Run from repository root (workspace root)
echo "Before cleanup:"
lsof -i tcp:8011 || true
lsof -i tcp:5287 || true

# Graceful stop first
lsof -ti tcp:8011 | xargs -r kill -15
lsof -ti tcp:5287 | xargs -r kill -15
sleep 1

# If still running, force stop
lsof -ti tcp:8011 | xargs -r kill -9
lsof -ti tcp:5287 | xargs -r kill -9

echo "After cleanup:"
lsof -i tcp:8011 || true
lsof -i tcp:5287 || true
```

### 2) Set startup preferences mode (optional, DEV only)

In DEV mode the app can start with either factory defaults or your last saved
preferences.  A **dev prefs mode** flag stored in the app's localStorage
controls this.  The flag is initialised to `"defaults"` the very first time you
launch the app (no stored value → factory defaults).

Tip: To run JavaScript (e.g. `localStorage` commands): press **Cmd+Option+I** (macOS) or
**F12** to open the WebKit Web Inspector, then use its Console tab.
This keyboard shortcut is available in `desktop:dev` builds only.

Switch to **factory defaults** (useful for testing first-run behaviour):

```{js}
// Run in the Developer Dock console, then restart the app.
localStorage.setItem("x2chess.devPrefsMode", "defaults")
```

Switch to **your last saved preferences**:

```{js}
// Run in the Developer Dock console, then restart the app.
localStorage.setItem("x2chess.devPrefsMode", "user")
```

Check the current setting:

```{js}
localStorage.getItem("x2chess.devPrefsMode")   // "defaults" | "user" | null
```

> Note: This flag is only read in DEV builds.  PROD builds always restore saved
> user preferences and ignore this key.

### 3) Start Tauri desktop client (Terminal A)

Two launch modes are available.  Choose the one that fits your session:

**Isolated mode** — webview storage lives in `run/DEV/webview-data/` (under the repository root, separate from any
installed PROD build).  Recommended for normal development.

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm run desktop:dev:isolated
```

**Shared mode** — webview storage uses the OS default path (same location a packaged
PROD build of the same bundle ID would use).  Use this when you need to test against
real accumulated user data, or to verify a migration on an existing install.

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm run desktop:dev
```

> Note: Both scripts start their own frontend dev server on port `5287`.
> Note: `run/DEV/webview-data/` (under the repository root) is created automatically on first launch if it does not exist.
> Tip: To open the AST panel: open the app sidebar, enable the **Developer Tools** checkbox, then click **Open Developer Dock**.
> Tip: Use menu controls **Save mode** (`Autosave`/`Manual`) and **Save now** for the active game tab.

**Dev-mode — all debug output** — pass `VITE_DEBUG_MODULES=*` before the npm script.  All modules emit
`log.debug` lines.  This is the default behaviour when no filter is set, so this variant is mainly useful for
overriding a narrower filter that was previously stored in localStorage.

```{bash}
cd frontend
VITE_DEBUG_MODULES='*' npm run desktop:dev:isolated
```

**Dev-mode — one module only** — pass `VITE_DEBUG_MODULES=<moduleName>` to restrict debug output to a single
JS module (use the exact string passed as the first argument to `log.debug` in that module).

```{bash}
cd frontend
VITE_DEBUG_MODULES=move_position npm run desktop:dev:isolated
```

Multiple modules can be enabled by separating names with commas:
`VITE_DEBUG_MODULES=useAppStartup,board`.

> Tip: The filter can also be changed at runtime without restarting.  Open the WebKit inspector console
> (Cmd+Option+I in `desktop:dev` builds) and call:
>
> ```javascript
> // Enable all modules
> setDebugModules("*")
> // Enable one module
> setDebugModules("useAppStartup")
> // Clear the runtime override (reverts to the env-var value or the default)
> setDebugModules(null)
> ```
>
> The setting is persisted in localStorage and survives restarts.  `VITE_DEBUG_MODULES` (env var) is only read
> when there is no localStorage override.

#### Debugging with breakpoints

Launch the app normally:

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm run desktop:dev:isolated
```

Open the inspector with **Cmd+Option+I** (macOS) inside the running app window.

**Setting breakpoints:**

1. In the inspector, go to the **Sources** tab.
2. Press **Cmd+P** to open the file picker and type the source file name (e.g. `pgn_runtime.ts`).  Vite serves original TypeScript source via source maps, so you can set breakpoints directly on `.ts` lines.
3. Click the line number gutter to toggle a breakpoint.  An orange/blue marker confirms it is active.
4. Trigger the code path in the app.  Execution pauses at the breakpoint; the **Scope** panel shows local variables and the **Call Stack** panel shows the full trace.

**Catching early-initialization code (inspector open before JS runs):**

Pass `X2CHESS_OPEN_DEVTOOLS=1` — the Rust setup hook opens the WebKit inspector automatically before the first JS instruction executes:

```{bash}
cd frontend
X2CHESS_OPEN_DEVTOOLS=1 npm run desktop:dev:isolated
```

The inspector window appears alongside the app window at launch.  Set your breakpoints in the **Sources** tab immediately; then resume execution (▶) to let the app start.

> Note: `X2CHESS_OPEN_DEVTOOLS` is only honoured in debug builds (`tauri dev`).  It has no effect in production builds.

> Tip: For code paths that are easy to trigger interactively (not startup-only), the `debugger;` statement is simpler — add it to the source, save, let Vite HMR push the change, then trigger the code path with the inspector already open.

#### Resetting isolated dev storage

Delete the directory — the app recreates it with factory defaults on the next launch:

```{bash}
rm -rf run/DEV/webview-data
```

#### Using a custom storage path

Pass `X2CHESS_WEBVIEW_DATA` directly to redirect storage anywhere:

```{bash}
cd frontend
X2CHESS_WEBVIEW_DATA=/path/to/my/profile tauri dev
```

## Useful Scripts

### Install/update dependencies

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm install
```

### Build frontend for production

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm run build
```

## Testing

### Run unit tests in a top-level directory

```{bash}
cd frontend
npm test
```

### Run unit tests of a single file

Run a single test by name (--test-name-pattern accepts a regex):

```{bash}
cd frontend
node --test --import tsx --test-name-pattern "parses NAGs" "../parts/pgnparser/test/pgn_model.test.ts"
```

### Run unit tests with DEBUG output

Run with debug output (prints data for every test that calls `debugTest`):

```{bash}
cd frontend
DEBUG_TEST=1 node --test --import tsx --test-name-pattern "FEN.*3\." "../parts/pgnparser/test/pgn_model.test.ts"
```

### Run regression tests (targeted)

```{bash}
cd frontend
node --test --import tsx test/app_shell/dev_dock_regression.test.ts
```

### Stop local dev services quickly

```{bash}
lsof -ti tcp:5287 | xargs -r kill -9
```
